schools.summary: Gill: done not by the whole body but by one, yet, not being discovered, it was imputed to all.
Keil & Delitzsch: Achan taking of the accursed thing, the anger of the LORD kindled.

themes: Israel committed a trespass in the accursed thing... and the anger of the LORD was kindled
one man's secret sin bringing guilt on all
a little leaven leaveneth the whole lump (1 Cor. 5:6)

schools.summary: Gill: Ai was the next city of importance, smaller than Jericho, a few miles distant; the men went up and viewed it.

themes: Joshua sent men from Jericho to Ai... Go up and view the country
prudence undertaken under a hidden curse
except the LORD keep the city, the watchman wakes in vain (Ps. 127:1)

schools.summary: Gill: after reconnoitring they returned and gave it as their opinion that the whole army need not go up.
Keil & Delitzsch: the counsel to send two or three thousand.

themes: Let not all the people go up; but let about two or three thousand men go up
self-confidence overlooking dependence on God
let him that thinketh he standeth take heed lest he fall (1 Cor. 10:12)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua detached the largest number proposed, that there might be strength enough; they went to the very gate, and fled before the men of Ai.

themes: there went up... about three thousand men: and they fled before the men of Ai
the rout exposing that God was not with them
ye shall flee when none pursueth you (Lev. 26:17)

schools.summary: Gill: their loss was small, but their shame and disgrace great — a sufficient rebuke of providence.
Clarke: they were chased from the gate even unto Shebarim.

themes: the men of Ai smote of them about thirty and six men... the hearts of the people melted, and became as water
the small loss a great rebuke, courage broken
the bold made as water when God withdraws (Prov. 28:1)

schools.summary: Gill: the rending of clothes was usual on hearing bad news, expressing grief and trouble.
Henry: Joshua, as a public person, interested himself more than any in the calamity.

themes: Joshua rent his clothes, and fell to the earth... before the ark of the LORD... and put dust upon their heads
humbling himself before God in calamity
humble yourselves under the mighty hand of God (1 Pet. 5:6)

schools.summary: Gill: the cry — what a distressed condition are we in! who could have thought this would be our case?
Keil & Delitzsch: the lament, wherefore hast thou brought us over Jordan.

themes: wherefore hast thou at all brought this people over Jordan... to destroy us?
the cry of faith mingled with the murmuring of distress
why art thou cast down? hope thou in God (Ps. 42:11)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua knew not what to say for the comfort of Israel, or in vindication of God's power against the charge of weakness brought by their enemies.
Wesley: his being at a loss what to say.

themes: O Lord, what shall I say, when Israel turneth their backs before their enemies!
the godly's grief chiefly for the dishonour to God
what wilt thou do unto thy great name? (Josh. 7:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the Canaanites on the east and west who would hear.
Keil & Delitzsch: the fear they would environ and cut off their name, and the appeal to God's great name.

themes: the Canaanites... shall environ us round, and cut off our name... and what wilt thou do unto thy great name?
the surest ground of prayer God's own glory
that he act for his name's sake (Exod. 32:12)

schools.summary: Gill: said not as refusing his supplication but as encouraging and strengthening him, and to instruct and prepare him for what was to be done.

themes: Get thee up; wherefore liest thou thus upon thy face?
not a rebuke of prayer but a call to act
wherefore criest thou? speak that they go forward (Exod. 14:15)

schools.summary: Gill: though one had committed it, others might have known and connived; there was sin among them, to be discovered and punished.
Keil & Delitzsch: Israel's sin, transgressing the covenant.

themes: Israel hath sinned... they have even taken of the accursed thing, and have also stolen, and dissembled also
the manifold wickedness in one act, sin upon sin
be sure your sin will find you out (Num. 32:23)

schools.summary: Gill: they could not stand, being forsaken of God for the sin committed among them.
Keil & Delitzsch: the turning of their backs because they were accursed.

themes: neither will I be with you any more, except ye destroy the accursed from among you
unpurged sin forfeiting God's favour
purge out therefore the old leaven (1 Cor. 5:7)

schools.summary: Gill: 'up' signifies to bestir himself and be active, first to sanctify the people; there was an accursed thing in the midst, and they could not stand till it was taken away.

themes: sanctify the people... there is an accursed thing in the midst of thee... until ye take away the accursed thing
the dealing with sin beginning in self-consecration
cleanse ourselves from all filthiness (2 Cor. 7:1)

schools.summary: Gill: brought the next morning before Joshua, the elders, and probably the tabernacle.
Keil & Delitzsch: the lot narrowing from tribe to family to household to man.

themes: ye shall be brought according to your tribes... and the household... man by man
God himself finding out the hidden offender
the lot disposed of by the LORD (Prov. 16:33)

schools.summary: Gill: burning was one of the four capital punishments with the Jews, ordered here because the accursed city was to be burnt; he had transgressed the covenant and wrought folly in Israel.

themes: he that is taken with the accursed thing shall be burnt with fire... because he hath wrought folly in Israel
sin both guilt and folly
the wages of sin is death (Rom. 6:23)

schools.summary: Gill: his rising early showed his readiness and diligence to obey God's command.
Henry: the discovery of Achan by the lot, which proved a perfect detection.

themes: Joshua rose up early... and the tribe of Judah was taken
the relentless certainty of God's discovery
nothing covered that shall not be revealed (Matt. 10:26)

schools.summary: Gill: the several families in the tribe, the heads of them.
Keil & Delitzsch: the family of the Zarhites taken, then Zabdi.

themes: he took the family of the Zarhites... and Zabdi was taken
the lot narrowing toward one man
our secret sins in the light of thy countenance (Ps. 90:8)

schools.summary: Gill: the household of Zabdi, the heads of each house therein; Achan was taken.

themes: he brought his household man by man; and Achan... was taken
the guilty at last singled out
spoken in darkness shall be heard in the light (Luke 12:3)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua treated him in a humane, affectionate, respectable manner, though so great a criminal.
Clarke: Joshua urged him, now detected, to give glory to God by confession.

themes: My son, give... glory to the LORD God of Israel, and make confession unto him
confession giving glory to God, justifying the Judge
that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest (Ps. 51:4)

schools.summary: Gill: he made a free and open confession of his sin.
Keil & Delitzsch: him acknowledging his sin and confessing it.

themes: Indeed I have sinned against the LORD God of Israel, and thus and thus have I done
full and free confession owning the offence to God
he that confesseth and forsaketh them shall have mercy (Prov. 28:13)

schools.summary: Gill: a goodly Babylonish garment, made in Babylon (Shinar), much esteemed; with the silver and wedge of gold; he coveted, took, and hid them in his tent.

themes: When I saw... I coveted them, and took them; and... they are hid in the earth in the midst of my tent
the whole progress of sin from eye to deed
lust conceived bringeth forth sin and death (James 1:14-15)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua sent messengers directly to Achan's tent, to see if it was as he had said. Calvin: the haste shown, the messengers running, displaying their zeal.

themes: So Joshua sent messengers, and they ran unto the tent; and... it was hid in his tent
the hidden sin unearthed and proved
there is nothing hid which shall not be manifested (Mark 4:22)

schools.summary: Gill: they took them out of the place, hole, or pit in which they were hid.
Keil & Delitzsch: them brought to Joshua and all Israel, laid out before the LORD.

themes: they took them out of the midst of the tent... and laid them out before the LORD
the offence laid before him against whom it was done
all things are naked and opened unto his eyes (Heb. 4:13)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua and all Israel are mentioned, to show the agreement between them in the punishment.
Clarke: Achan and all that was his taken to the valley.

themes: Joshua, and all Israel with him, took Achan... and brought them unto the valley of Achor
the whole nation united to purge the sin
ye shall put away the evil from among you (Deut. 13:5)

schools.summary: Gill: Joshua asked why he had been the occasion of so much trouble by committing this sin.
Keil & Delitzsch: the LORD troubling him, and all Israel stoning and burning.

themes: Why hast thou troubled us? the LORD shall trouble thee this day. And all Israel stoned him
the troubler of Israel troubled by the LORD
his mischief shall return upon his own head (Ps. 7:16)

schools.summary: Gill: the heap of stones raised at the place of his execution as a monument; the LORD turned from his anger, and the place was named the valley of Achor.

themes: they raised over him a great heap of stones... So the LORD turned from the fierceness of his anger... The valley of Achor
the purging of sin restoring God's favour
the valley of Achor for a door of hope (Hos. 2:15)
